assessor_1_raw: Strong experiment design for ticket escalation prediction. Operational label definition avoids annotation subjectivity. Temporal split with product-line stratification is appropriate. Both models receive equal tuning effort (20 trials/combinations). PR-AUC is the right metric for imbalanced binary classification. Secondary metrics including ECE, precision at fixed recall, and per-product-line false positive rates are comprehensive. Agent leakage prevention via aggregate statistics is well-designed. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_2_raw: The transformer escalation prediction experiment is well-structured. The temporal split mirrors deployment. Feature preprocessing is correctly fit on training only. The transformer's sequence modeling and timing information are additional capabilities relative to the BoW baseline, making this a fair test of architectural advantage. Per-product-line stratification enables confound detection. Bootstrap confidence intervals are included for uncertainty quantification. Verdict: defense_wins.
assessor_3_raw: The experiment correctly identifies PR-AUC as superior to ROC-AUC for imbalanced escalation prediction. The 36-month dataset provides substantial training signal. Sequence truncation to 512 tokens is a standard BERT limitation. The equal tuning budget (20 combinations for each model) is well-specified. The scope is appropriately limited to the same SaaS environment and support process. No methodological flaws. Verdict: defense_wins.
adjudication_raw: Three independent assessors unanimously reached defense_wins. The experiment is consistently evaluated as methodologically sound.
